what_input_feature: A 4-byte device-class / profile-class signature at offset 0x0c that the parser maps to XYZ-data PCS (winner seeds carry 'spac'=73 70 61 63 or 'prtr'=70 72 74 72), combined with the Lab output PCS selection, so InPCS==cmsSigXYZData and OutPCS==cmsSigLabData reach line 443.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds set offset 0x0c-0x0f to 'spac'/'prtr' (W bytes 70(p)/73(s) at 0x0c) and additionally flip the discriminator bytes at 0x2d (W=01) and 0x3d (W=50 'P'), which the loser never produces; these literal four-byte FOURCC class tags are exactly what I2SRandReplace can splice from a logged CMP operand.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are stuck at offset 0x0c='mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) for all 20 occurrences and keep 0x2d=00, 0x3d=00, so the parser routes to a PCS path that never enters the XYZ->Lab arm at line 443.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the parser compared the input class tag against the literal 'spac'/'prtr' FOURCC constants and substitutes that constant into the input bytes; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact multi-byte tag, so value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S axis.

what_input_feature: Same XYZ-PCS-in / Lab-PCS-out structural requirement, but reached via incremental gradient progress on the multi-field colorspace comparison rather than a single substitution; the input must simultaneously satisfy the class tag and the colorspace fields (0x10-0x16) selecting Lab output.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 with hitcount 19 on the branch, getting CMP_MAP gradient credit for each colorspace/PCS sub-field that moves closer to the XYZ->Lab discriminant, layered on top of I2S substitution.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value profile) resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 1, so without the Hamming/prefix-distance feedback it rarely accumulates the multi-field colorspace state needed to enter the XYZ->Lab arm.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient preserves intermediate inputs that get closer on the colorspace/PCS comparisons, giving the fuzzer a smooth path to the XYZ->Lab discriminant; cmplog lacks this distance feedback, so value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog on the value_profile axis.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte device-class signature at offset 0x0c must equal a 4CC such as 'spac' or 'prtr' (and the colorspace fields at 0x10-0x16 must form a profile whose ColorSpaceIn is compatible with CurrentColorSpace) so the loop routes into the devicelink/abstract branch (line 576) and reaches the AddConversion call at line 592 with a non-NULL Lut.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'spac' (73 70 61 63) or 'prtr' (70 72 74 72) at offset 0x0c-0x0f and resolve the true branch with hitcount 19, taking the True side of line 592 (W T=12).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at offset 0x0c-0x0f, never take the True side of line 592 (L T=0 F=38), and stay blocked with hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile: the CMP interception logs the literal 4CC operands compared against the input (device-class / colorspace tag matches) and substitutes them at the matching input offsets, planting the exact 'spac'/'prtr' magic that value_profile's Hamming-bucket gradient alone never completes; the winning seed's lineage shows an I2S-floor dash row confirming an I2S find in the ancestor chain.

what_input_feature: The same device-class/colorspace byte configuration (device-class 4CC at 0x0c != 'mntr', compatible colorspace at 0x10-0x16) that routes into the devicelink branch and satisfies the ColorSpaceIsCompatible check at line 568, reaching AddConversion at line 592.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 with hitcount 19; its seeds reach the True side of the blocker (line 592 W T=12) after passing the colorspace-compatibility chain.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 8/10 with avg hitcount 1, far below the winner, indicating its plain I2S substitution rarely assembles the full compatible-colorspace configuration the branch requires.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient over cmplog: each CMP-operand pair closer to matching adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus, giving a stepwise gradient toward the colorspace-compatibility comparisons that bare cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot climb, so vpc compounds I2S substitution with gradient retention to reach the branch reliably.

what_input_feature: Profile-header bytes (device class FOURCC at offset 0x0c and color-space signatures at 0x10/0x14) that select a non-input, non-devicelink profile so the output-direction branch (line 602) runs _cmsReadOutputLUT and a subsequent ComputeConversion/AddConversion failure jumps to Error while Lut is non-NULL.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry varied device-class FOURCCs (link/prtr/spac) and color spaces (MCHC/GRAY/RGB/MCH1/Lab) at the header offsets, and per-role coverage shows the winner exclusively hitting the output-direction block (lines 562-566, 602-612) and the abstract/conversion path (583-592).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are uniformly 'mntr'+'RGB ' headers that take the input direction (line 597 lIsInput true), so Lut is freed at line 619 before any goto and the Error path never sees a non-NULL Lut.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes intercepted CMP operands (the FOURCC/colorspace literals compared inside cmsGetDeviceClass/ColorSpaceIsCompatible) into the input bytes, letting vpc construct the rare device-class/colorspace combination that drives the output-direction conversion failure; plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace.

what_input_feature: The same output-direction-failure profile, but here reached via gradient progress on the integer/string CMPs guarding the colorspace-compatibility and conversion checks rather than purely by substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 80 on the branch; its CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to matching the colorspace signature CMPs (lines 568, 583), so the output-direction conversion path is reached and fails into Error.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 8; without CMP_MAP-bucket feedback it cannot retain near-miss inputs on the colorspace comparisons, so it rarely assembles the exact header combination that drives the output-direction failure.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) preserves inputs that approach matching the colorspace/device-class comparisons as new corpus entries, supplying cmplog's substitution stage in vpc with stepping-stones; cmplog alone has only edge-bucket feedback and cannot ratchet toward the rare combination.

what_input_feature: A profile that parses far enough to read a tag back (device class 'link'/'abst', valid acsp magic, a non-Lut16 LUT tag) so that _cmsGetTagTrueType returns a tag type signature OTHER than cmsSigLut16Type. This requires several 4-byte signature fields (device class at 0x0c, colorspace fields at 0x10-0x17, and the tag-true-type discriminator) to hold specific structural values rather than the still-blocked 'mntr'/Lut16 layout.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 trials and reaches the true side (avg hitcount 14, blocked only 6.5h). Its winning seeds carry device-class bytes 'link' (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and varied colorspace signatures, and offset 0x2d flips to 01 vs the loser's 00 — distinct multi-byte signature substitutions consistent with I2SRandReplace splicing a logged tag-type/signature operand into the input at the offsets the parser compares.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves 0/10, stays blocked all 24h with hitcount 0 on the branch. Its blocking seeds keep device class 'mntr' (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and offset 0x2d=00, so the tag read either fails or yields a Lut16 (mft2) true type, leaving OriginalType == cmsSigLut16Type and the true branch unreached.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile. I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the input bytes matching one operand of an intercepted signature/type comparison, and substitutes the other operand, planting the exact 4-byte signature(s) the parser needs to make _cmsGetTagTrueType return a non-Lut16 type. value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient cannot place an exact multi-byte signature, so it never crosses.

what_input_feature: The same non-Lut16-true-type profile structure, but reached via incremental Hamming/prefix-distance progress on the chain of signature/equality comparisons (device class, colorspace, tag type) so that intermediate near-matches are retained as corpus and refined toward the exact non-Lut16 layout.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 vs cmplog's 2/10 (prob_div 0.70, hit_div 8); its CMP_MAP feedback preserves seeds whose 4-byte signature fields (0x0c-0x12) are progressively closer to a valid non-Lut16 tag layout, building the corpus that the I2S stage then completes.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked ~20h with branch hitcount 6 — it has I2S but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient, so near-miss signature seeds that don't change an edge bucket are discarded and the search loses the partial-progress scaffold value_profile_cmplog keeps.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient over cmplog. Each signature/type CMP writes a (PC, distance-bucket) entry; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus. This retains the intermediate near-match profiles that cmplog throws away, giving the combined fuzzer a refinable foothold on the multi-field signature gate.

what_input_feature: Bytes that survive ICC header signature/colour-space checks and place a non-zero offset payload so the matrix stage's Data->Offset pointer is non-NULL; W seeds carry the 'lams'/'link' profile class and non-zero bytes at offsets 0x2d (01) and 0x38 (16) where the loser holds zero.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds reach the true side: avg hitcount on branch winner=29, loser=0, and the byte diff shows winner-only 01 at 0x2d and 16 at 0x38 (loser is 00 at both), the dataflow that yields a populated Offset.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile never resolves (resolved=0/10, blocked=10); its seeds hold 00 at 0x2d and 0x38 and the 'lcms'/'mntr' header bytes, so the matrix stage is built without an Offset and the true branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching ICC magic/signature operands into the input at the right offsets, letting value_profile_cmplog clear the header CMPs that gate construction of an offset-bearing matrix stage; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place those operands.

what_input_feature: Same offset-bearing matrix-stage requirement, but reached by gradually closing the distance on the ICC header/signature comparisons rather than by exact substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 vs cmplog 2/10, with branch hitcount winner=29 vs loser=13; the CMP_MAP gradient buckets let it ratchet toward the signature/colour-space bytes (0x05, 0x0c-0x0f) that select an offset-bearing stage.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 8/10 and stays at hitcount 13 with avg blocked duration 20.20h; without CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback it has no gradient to preserve near-miss inputs and rarely assembles the offset payload.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP and preserves any input that gets a comparand pair closer to matching; this gradient feedback, present only in value_profile_cmplog within this pair, drives the winner past the header gates that cmplog (edge-bucket only) cannot incrementally solve.

what_input_feature: A valid 4-byte ICC profile-class FOURCC at offset 0x0c (e.g. 'link','prtr','spac','lcms') plus a recognized device/colorspace tag, so DefaultICCintents builds a non-empty Lut whose stages cmsPipelineCat then dups into l1 via cmsPipelineInsertStage.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-default profile-class signatures at 0x0c-0x0f ('lcms','link','prtr','spac','MCH1'), and the vpc winner's lineage contains an I2S-floor dash row that splices logged CMP operands into these FOURCC offsets, producing inputs that traverse the stage-dup loop and take the blocked true side.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its loser-blocking seeds are uniformly 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f (6d 6e 74 72) and never substitute the alternate profile-class operands needed, so the pipeline stays in a shape that does not reach the InsertStage true branch (0/10 resolved).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the logged comparand for the profile-class FOURCC check at the input offset, which value_profile (no I2S stage) cannot perform.

what_input_feature: The same multi-profile FOURCC/colorspace fields, but reached via incremental Hamming-distance progress on the profile-class and colorspace CMP operands rather than a single exact substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient on top of I2S, so partial matches on the 0x0c-0x16 tag bytes earn new CMP_MAP buckets and are retained as corpus, letting it resolve 9/10 with 50 hits versus cmplog's 5 hits.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP feedback; without per-bucket distance credit it cannot accumulate the partial FOURCC/colorspace progress and resolves only 2/10 trials (loser blocked=8).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) rewards inputs that bring the profile-class/colorspace comparands closer to matching, a feedback signal cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: The input must place the literal name3 byte sequence (e.g. an entity/element name from the DTD) at the offset the parser interns, so that after dict interning the stored entry->name3 and the queried name3 are the same pointer.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog runs I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CMP buffer, finds where one comparison operand appears in the input, and substitutes the matching operand byte-for-byte, directly placing the required name3 literal (resolved 8/10).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot copy the exact operand into the input and must rely on havoc luck, staying blocked 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in vpc, absent in value_profile) substitutes the logged CMP operand into the input to satisfy the name comparison feeding the dict-interned name3 identity; this I2S delta is the only difference between the two fuzzers.

what_input_feature: The input must progressively close the gap on the upstream name comparisons (xmlStrEqual / interning compares) so the parser stores then re-queries an identical name3, requiring near-match operand bytes to be retained as corpus.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient: inputs that produce a CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any seen before are preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient toward the exact name match that cmplog alone does not retain (resolved 8/10).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-match inputs that don't trigger a new edge are discarded; it lacks the distance-bucket signal and stays blocked 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: the CMP_MAP gradient feedback (value_profile, present in vpc and absent in plain cmplog) preserves progressively-closer operand matches as corpus entries, enabling convergence on the exact name3 string; this value_profile delta separates the pair.

what_input_feature: Bytes that drive the libxml2 parser to allocate an XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE, i.e. a recognizable multi-byte '<![CDATA[' literal embedded in a well-formed document so the saver's switch dispatches to case XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE at line 988.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 and actually executes the CDATA arm (entry-line hits>=1; the W column shows L988 onward marked [W]); the CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves intermediate inputs that get progressively closer to matching the parser's multi-byte token comparisons, building the '<![CDATA[' / '<?xml' / '<!DOCTYPE' skeleton seen in the winning seed.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 and is blocked 9/10; lacking the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback it gets no reward for partial matches of the multi-byte CDATA token, so it never reliably assembles the literal and the saver dispatches to other case arms (L988 stays False=194 on the L side).
mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique is the value_profile CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient unique to value_profile_cmplog over cmplog; it rewards inputs whose CMP operands edge toward matching the parser's CDATA/element token comparisons, letting the corpus climb toward a valid '<![CDATA[' construct that cmplog (no CMP_MAP) cannot ratchet toward.

what_input_feature: The same requirement: bytes parsing into a tree with an XML_CDATA_SECTION_NODE, which depends on placing the exact multi-byte literal '<![CDATA[' (and surrounding well-formed XML) into the input.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10; its I2SRandReplace mutator reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and splices the exact comparand the parser tests for (the CDATA/element token bytes) directly into the input at matching offsets, so the precise '<![CDATA[' literal appears verbatim. The winning seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, confirming an I2S find.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves only 2/10 and is blocked 8/10; it has no I2SRandReplace mutator (havoc+token only), so it must stumble onto the exact multi-byte CDATA literal by chance and rarely does, leaving the saver to dispatch to other case arms.
mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique is cmplog's I2SRandReplace input-to-state substitution present in value_profile_cmplog but absent in value_profile; it copies the logged CDATA/element comparand into the input at matching offsets, directly synthesizing the literal token the parser needs to build a CDATA node.

what_input_feature: Input must produce an element node that already has a first namespace (node->nsDef != NULL) and then declare a SECOND, distinctly-prefixed namespace on the same element, requiring well-formed multi-byte tokens like "<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>", "<!DOCTYPE", and a parseable xmlns attribute chain so SAX2 calls xmlNewNs twice on the same node.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner seeds carry an intact, in-order XML prologue ("<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>", "<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM") at offsets 0x08+, which the I2SRandReplace mutator assembled by substituting CMP operands; the prologue parses far enough to drive a second xmlNewNs call and take the false (non-NULL nsDef) side, hitting it 6 times on average.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds are shorter and structurally mangled (e.g. 97/156-byte seeds with byte runs like 0x13 repeats and corrupted prologue ordering), so the parser at most reaches the first namespace declaration; the loser only resolves the branch in 2/10 trials with avg hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and splices logged comparison operands (XML keyword/token bytes) into the input at matching offsets, repairing the multi-byte structural tokens that gate reaching a second xmlNewNs call; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct those exact token sequences.

what_input_feature: Same structural target (an element whose node->nsDef is already non-NULL when a second xmlns is declared), but here the winning edge is value-profile gradient feedback that preserves intermediate inputs whose CMP operands are progressively closer to matching the XML structural literals, letting the corpus climb toward a parseable two-namespace document.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 trials versus cmplog's 0/10; the CMP_MAP bucket feedback retains near-miss inputs (Hamming/prefix-distance closer to the xmlns and prologue token comparisons), so the corpus accumulates the structural progress needed to reach and append the second namespace at the blocker false side (avg hitcount winner=6, loser=0).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the same I2SRandReplace mutator but only the edge-bucket feedback signal; it never retains the gradient intermediates, so across all 10 trials it stays blocked (resolved=0/10, blocked=10) and the branch hitcount is 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a distance-bucket into CMP_MAP, and an input producing an operand pair closer to matching than any seen before is preserved as new coverage; this gradient is the only technique differing between vpc and cmplog and is what lets the corpus accumulate toward the two-namespace document.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < all_names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: A name-table header whose count/stringOffset fields and trailing NameRecord array survive hb_sanitize_context_t so that all_names.length (= this->table->count) is non-zero; the structured header tags ('typ1', 'name', table fourccs like 'GB') must match the values the parser compares against.
why_winner_satisfies: The VPC winner-resolving seed begins with structured header bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1') and contains 'name' and a fourcc-like 'GB' run, and resolves the branch (resolved=9/10) hitting the loop body 13 times on average; its lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted a logged CMP operand (a magic/fourcc constant) into the header so sanitization passed.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 — its seeds never produce a sanitization-passing non-zero count; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot copy the exact compared header constant into the input and stays stuck at the false (zero-length) side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog has the I2SRandReplace mutator that reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the other operand of an intercepted integer/string CMP at matching offsets, planting the exact header constant the name-table sanitizer demands; plain value_profile lacks this mutator, so this pair's win is attributable to I2S.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < all_names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: Incrementally improving header bytes that approach a valid count/offset relationship; the CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose compared operands get closer to matching, providing intermediate corpus stepping stones toward a sanitization-passing non-zero NameRecord count.
why_winner_satisfies: The VPC winner resolves 9/10 vs cmplog's 1/10 and reaches 13 hits vs 2; VPC adds the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback on top of cmplog's I2S, so partial-match header states are preserved as corpus and combined with I2S substitution to clear the sanitizer.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount only 2 — it has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient, so near-miss header inputs that improve operand-distance are not retained as new coverage and the search has no gradient toward a valid non-zero-count table.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose CMP operands are closer to matching than any seen adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, giving a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient toward a passing header that plain cmplog (no CMP_MAP) lacks; this pair's win is attributable to value_profile.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: A name-table header whose magic/structural tags (the 'name' sfnt tag and NameRecord platform/encoding/nameID/language CMP fields) match the values harfbuzz tests at parse time, so all_names is non-empty and at least one entry survives into names.length, making the line-504 loop take its true side.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed carries literal sfnt-table tags ('typ1', 'name', and GB/G tag bytes) at the table-directory offsets, populating NameRecords so names.length becomes 12 and the loop iterates; the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser never plants those exact structural tags; its name table parses to zero usable records, names.length stays 0, and the loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds where the program compared the input against the expected sfnt tag / NameRecord constants, and substitutes those exact constants into the input — value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact tag bytes, so vpc beats vp on this axis.

source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->names.length; i++)
what_input_feature: The same structural name-table fields, but reached incrementally: byte sequences progressively closer to the expected platform/encoding/language constants so that near-matching inputs are retained and refined into a fully-parseable record set with names.length>0.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner accretes the right structural bytes; against plain cmplog the winner resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 12 versus the loser's 1/10 and hitcount 2, the extra gain coming from CMP_MAP gradient retention of partial matches.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partially-correct intermediates are not preserved as corpus and the path to a complete parseable record set is not climbed; cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount only 2.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching the expected name-table constants, letting near-matching intermediates be refined toward names.length>0; plain cmplog lacks this gradient retention, so vpc beats cmplog on this axis.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte 'CFF2' table tag (0x43 0x46 0x46 0x32) present at the table-directory entry (offset 0x0c in the winning seeds) so that reference_table<cff2> resolves to a real table rather than the Null pool.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII tag 'CFF2' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (43 46 46 32) which the directory parser matches to find the table; the false branch (cff2 != Null) and downstream top-dict parsing are then exercised.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile-blocking seeds carry different bytes at 0x0c-0x0f (e.g. 47 50 4f 53 = 'GPOS' / other tags) so reference_table<cff2> returns the Null sentinel, the true branch is taken, and execution jumps to fail.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver tag-equality compare and substitutes the required 'CFF2' literal into the seed at the matching offset; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only buckets Hamming distance, so it cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte tag — hence vpc beats vp.

what_input_feature: A table-directory and CFF2 header byte layout (offset/length fields around 0x00-0x1f) progressively shaped toward a sanitizable structure, where partial-match progress on multi-byte offset/length compares earns corpus retention.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds show structured header fields (e.g. 00 01 00 00 00 01 sfnt version and offset bytes 00 00 00 18 / 02) that the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient rewards as it approaches a valid directory, letting the winner cross to the false branch within 7.10h.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog-blocking seeds remain blocked (9/10) for 22.20h because cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback; without distance-bucket coverage credit it cannot incrementally retain near-valid header layouts, so the directory/offset structure is never assembled even when the tag is solvable.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient grants new-coverage credit for inputs whose offset/length operands are Hamming-closer to a sanitizable layout, steering the corpus toward the valid structure; cmplog lacks this feedback, so vpc (which has both) beats cmplog.

what_input_feature: Multi-byte font/table magic literals at the input head (e.g. the 'loca' FOURCC table tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f and an SFNT version/magic at 0x00-0x03) that the parser compares verbatim before glyf->get_extents can read glyph outlines.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner-resolving seed carries 'loca' literally at 0x0c-0x0f (6c 6f 63 61) and 'typ1' at 0x00-0x03, exact byte runs that satisfy the equality comparisons gating the table parse, and its ancestor chain shows an I2S-floor dash row.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds carry no I2S stage, so they never substitute a logged comparison operand into the input; at offset 0x0c the loser bytes are scattered (47(G), 00, 01, 2c) rather than the 'loca' tag, so the table comparison fails and glyf returns no extents.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the bytes matching one operand of the table-tag comparison, and substitutes the expected 'loca'/magic operand at that offset; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace so it cannot land the multi-byte literal.

what_input_feature: The same concentrated magic/table-tag literals at offsets 0x00-0x0f, reached via a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient over the multi-byte comparison rather than by direct substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: vpc adds the CMP_MAP gradient: a near-match input that gets the table-tag comparison closer than any seen pair adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, ratcheting toward the 'loca'/magic literal that unlocks glyf->get_extents.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP bucket feedback, so partial-prefix progress on the multi-byte comparison earns no coverage credit and is discarded; cmplog resolved only 1/10 trials versus vpc's 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a matching-prefix-length bucket per comparison PC, rewarding inputs that approach the table-tag literal byte-by-byte; combined with I2S in vpc this gives a gradient cmplog alone lacks.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_get_glyph_from_name(hb_font_t*, void*, char const*, int, unsigned int*, void*)
what_input_feature: The literal 4-byte table tag 'post' (70 6f 73 74) placed at the table-directory offset (here 0x0c-0x0f), so the OpenType loader recognizes and parses a post table whose name lookup can succeed.
why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds contain the exact ASCII run 'post' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (70 6f 73 74) plus the 'true' magic at 0x00-0x03, and value_profile_cmplog resolves the true branch in 8/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds carry no 'post' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (they show 'kern'/'fff'/'erdn' style bytes there) and resolve the branch 0/10, because plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace to substitute the exact 'post' FOURCC into the directory offset.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the table-tag comparison against the literal 'post', and substitutes that operand into the seed at the directory offset; value_profile has no such substitution stage so it cannot place the exact 4-byte tag.

function: hb-ot-font.cc:hb_ot_get_glyph_from_name(hb_font_t*, void*, char const*, int, unsigned int*, void*)
what_input_feature: Beyond the 'post' tag, the input must supply post-table substructure (version/name-index/string bytes) close enough to a valid name entry that get_glyph_from_name returns a glyph; partial-match feedback is needed to incrementally home in on it.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog carries CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward inputs whose post-table CMP operands move closer to matching, and it resolves the branch 8/10 with avg hitcount 4 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP partial-match feedback, so it cannot preserve incremental progress toward a valid name entry and resolves only 1/10 trials (blocked 9).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) preserves corpus inputs that get closer to a satisfying post-table name comparison; cmplog lacks this feedback so partial progress is discarded between executions.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte table-directory tag must be the literal FOURCC 'OS/2' (bytes 4f 53 2f 32) so the OS/2 table is parsed and OS2->use_typo_metrics() && OS2->has_data() resolve true at line 82.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed places the exact ASCII run 'OS/2' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, the table tag harfbuzz compares against when locating the OS/2 table; the winner's ancestor chain carries an I2S-floor dash row.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser lacks I2SRandReplace, so it never substitutes the exact 'OS/2' magic tag and resolved=0/10 at the branch; its blocking seeds show no 'OS/2' tag at the directory offsets.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver tag comparison and substitutes the exact 'OS/2' FOURCC operand into the input, an operation value_profile cannot perform because it has no CmpLog buffer / I2S stage.

what_input_feature: Beyond the exact tag, the OS/2 table fields (sTypoAscender / use_typo flags and table length offsets) must be filled to multi-byte values that pass has_data() and the typo-metrics gate, requiring incremental refinement of the structured byte fields.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose OS/2 length/offset and flag comparisons get progressively closer, so it climbs to a structurally valid table (winner hitcount=4, resolved=9/10).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has no CMP_MAP distance feedback, so once the tag matches it cannot incrementally tune the remaining OS/2 length/flag fields; it resolved only 1/10 and avg hitcount=0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback preserves intermediate inputs that bring the OS/2 structural comparisons closer to matching, a gradient cmplog lacks, enabling the extra refinement past the bare tag match.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte post-table version field must equal the exact 32-bit constant 0x00020000 (bytes 00 02 00 00) at the version offset, so the equality test version != 0x00020000 fails and the function continues into v2 parsing instead of returning.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the version bytes (00 02 00 00 at offset 0x17-0x1a window: 18 00 02 00 00), and the winner resolves 10/10 with hitcount 39 on the branch; the I2S stage in value_profile_cmplog substitutes the logged comparison operand 0x00020000 into the input.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds do not place 0x00020000 at the version field (byte-diff offsets 0x17/0x19 show W=18/02 vs scattered L bytes), so version != 0x00020000 stays true and it blocks 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver entry for the version != 0x00020000 comparison and substitutes the constant operand 0x00020000 directly into the input; plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a coarse CMP_MAP distance bucket, which gives no gradient on an all-or-nothing 4-byte equality.

what_input_feature: The same 4-byte version field must reach 0x00020000; here the discriminating capability is the CMP_MAP closeness gradient that preserves intermediate inputs whose version operand is progressively nearer to the constant.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 (hitcount 39) versus cmplog's 2/10 (hitcount 3); the winner's extra CMP_MAP gradient bucket signal preserves near-miss version inputs as corpus, accelerating convergence to the exact 0x00020000 magic.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so it only resolves 2/10 and blocks 8/10 with avg duration 19.80h, lacking the closeness-bucket signal that rewards inputs approaching the constant.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming-distance buckets on the integer compare) gives new coverage credit to inputs whose version operand is closer to 0x00020000, retaining stepping-stones cmplog discards; this distance-bucket feedback is the technique cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte field at offset 0x18 holding the big-endian value 00 02 00 00 (0x00020000), the post-table version 2.0, matched exactly by the gate at line 223.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the bytes 00 02 00 00 at offsets 0x18-0x1b; the byte-diff shows offset 0x18 W=00x5, 0x19 W=02x5, 0x1a W=00x5, 0x1b W=00x5, exactly the 0x00020000 the version==0x00020000 gate requires.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot copy the intercepted 0x00020000 CMP operand into the input; its loser-blocking seeds carry arbitrary bytes at offset 0x18 (e.g. 0x1b loser=47(G)x8) and never satisfy the version-2.0 gate, blocking on the false side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the version==0x00020000 comparison and substitutes the recorded constant 0x00020000 at the matching input offset, which value_profile (no I2SRandReplace) cannot do.

what_input_feature: The same 4-byte version field at offset 0x18 must reach the 32-bit constant 0x00020000 checked by the line-223 gate.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient preserves intermediate inputs whose version field is progressively closer (smaller Hamming distance) to 0x00020000, and the winner resolves 10/10 with hitcount 39 on the branch versus cmplog's 2/10 and hitcount 3.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog alone has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-match version values are not retained as corpus and the search lacks the distance-bucket hill-climb; cmplog blocks 8/10 with avg duration 19.80h on this branch.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a (PC, operand-distance bucket) for the version==0x00020000 comparison; an input closer to 0x00020000 adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus, giving the vpc winner a gradient toward the constant that bare cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte post-table version field must equal the exact magic 0x00020000; the winner seeds carry version 0x0002 (bytes 00 02) where the loser seeds carry other values.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the post-table header and shows the byte run 00 02 00 00 at the version offset (0x18 region: '00 02 00 00 00 0a'), so version != 0x00020000 is false and the OR short-circuit does not fire.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds carry no I2S substitution stage, so they never copy the intercepted 0x00020000 comparison operand into the version offset; their byte sets at 0x18 are dominated by 00 and unrelated values, leaving version != 0x00020000 true.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the version-vs-0x00020000 CMP, finds the input bytes matching one operand, and substitutes the literal 0x00020000 into the input; value_profile lacks this mutator (I2S delta).

what_input_feature: Beyond matching the version magic, the glyph index must be brought in bounds (glyph < glyphNameIndex->len), which requires the CMP_MAP gradient feedback to preserve near-matching length/index comparisons.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner hits the false side 55089 times and proceeds into the array-indexing body (lines 258-272 marked [W]), indicating the glyph-vs-len bound was satisfied repeatedly under the combined CMP_MAP gradient signal.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 1820 versus the winner's 55089; without value_profile's CMP_MAP bucket feedback it cannot retain inputs that incrementally close the glyph>=glyphNameIndex->len bound comparison.

what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt header (00 01 00 00) plus a table directory containing cmap/GSUB so the font parses and get_nominal_glyph(0x0020) resolves; the magic 4-byte table tags ('cmap','GSUB') must appear verbatim.

why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) gets no Hamming/prefix gradient, so it cannot incrementally tune the codepoint/table bytes toward the space-fallback condition and resolves only 1/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch.

what_input_feature: A font blob whose table directory carries valid 4-byte FOURCC table tags (e.g. 'cmap', 'GSUB') at the directory offsets, so the shaper parses real tables and reaches the buffer state that sets the HAS_SPACE_FALLBACK scratch flag.

mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog has the CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile stage) which cmplog lacks; the Hamming/prefix-distance bucket rewards inputs whose CMP operands get closer to matching, preserving intermediate font-structure states as corpus so the parser eventually accepts the layout and the space-fallback flag is set.

what_input_feature: Specific glyph-category code bytes (e.g. the USE category literals in the 0x25/0x16/0x05 run) placed at the offsets the syllable machine reads, so the trans-action table dispatches to case 10.

what_input_feature: A font/tag input that drives the parsed script value to the HB_SCRIPT_INVALID sentinel (the switch operand at line 41 must equal the INVALID constant), reaching the case at line 43.

why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry language-like subtags ('uu-hans', '-u-hans') in the tail, the product of incremental gradient refinement; cmplog's I2S alone cannot climb such a multi-byte similarity gradient, but value_profile's CMP_MAP rewards each byte that brings the lookup operands closer.
